description: Analyze the NMR ensemble of human ubiquitin (PDB: 1D3Z, containing 10 conformers) to quantify per-residue backbone flexibility. Load the 1D3Z structure into PyMOL, align the 10 NMR states to a reference state, and evaluate per-residue backbone displacement/flexibility across the ensemble. Color the structure by per-residue flexibility to highlight regions of high thermal motion. Save a publication-quality PNG figure of the colored ensemble to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ubiquitin_ensemble.png. Additionally, create a plain-text report identifying the most flexible and rigid regions. In the report, list the top 5 most flexible residues with their associated numeric RMSD values, and explicitly mention the total number of models/states in the ensemble. Save the report to ~/PyMOL_Data/ubiquitin_flexibility_report.txt.
